zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Charron於大約1295年出生在英國時，她的父親Guiscard Charron26歲，母親Alice Lucy21歲。她於1310年與Bertram Monboucher II在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1342年之後在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Charron于大约1295年出生在英国时，她的父亲Guiscard Charron26岁，母亲Alice Lucy21岁。她于1310年与Bertram Monboucher II在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1342年之后在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: Charron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Donat, Lucien, Marcel, Alphonse, Emile, Laurent, Normand, Renald, Yves, Adrien.

French: metonymic occupational name for a cartwright, from Old French charron ‘cart’ (from Latin carro, genitive carronis, a derivative of carrum ‘cart’). Compare Charon , Sharon , Sharron , and Sharrow .

History: Pierre Charron dit Ducharme from Meaux in Seine-et-Marne, France, married Catherine Pillard in Montreal, QC, in 1665; see also Ducharme . Jean Charron dit Laferrière from Saintes in Charente-Maritime, France, married Anne d'Anneville in Quebec City, QC, in 1669; see also Laferriere . Charles Charron dit Larose and Cabanac from Chartres in Eure-et-Loir, France, married Élisabeth Poupard in Montreal, QC, in 1713.

variants.name: Charon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean-Yves, Francois, Valmore.
French: variant of   Charron   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Charron was born about 1295, in England, her father, Guiscard Charron, was 26 and her mother, Alice Lucy, was 21. She married Bertram Monboucher II in 1310, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died after 1342, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Charron est née environ 1295, à Angleterre, son père, Guiscard Charron, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Lucy, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Bertram Monboucher II en 1310, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée après 1342, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Charron um 1295 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guiscard Charron 26 und ihre Mutter Alice Lucy 21. Sie hat 1310 in England, United Kingdom Bertram Monboucher II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb nach 1342 in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Charron è nata il circa 1295 nel England, sua padre, Guiscard Charron, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Alice Lucy, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Bertram Monboucher II nel 1310, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì dopo il 1342, nel Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Charronが約1295年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Guiscard Charronは26歳，母Alice Lucyは21歳であった。1310年にイギリスイギリスでBertram Monboucher IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1342年以後にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Charron(이)가 대략 1295년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guiscard Charron(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Lucy(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1310년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Bertram Monboucher II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1342년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Charron nasceu aproximadamente 1295, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Guiscard Charron, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Alice Lucy, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Bertram Monboucher II em 1310, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu após 1342, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Charron родилась около 1295 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Guiscard Charron было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Lucy, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bertram Monboucher II в 1310 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла после 1342 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.
